***Kamikaze Girls*** ([Japanese](/wiki/Japanese_language "Japanese language"): 下妻物語　ヤンキーちゃんとロリータちゃん, [Hepburn](/wiki/Hepburn_romanization "Hepburn romanization"): *Shimotsuma Monogatari: Yankī-chan to Rorīta-chan*; lit. "Shimotsuma Story: Delinquent Girl and Lolita Girl"),[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5) is a 2002 Japanese novel written by [Novala Takemoto](/wiki/Novala_Takemoto "Novala Takemoto"). The story centers on the unlikely friendship between Momoko Ryugasaki, a "[Rococophile](/wiki/Rococo "Rococo")" and [Lolita](/wiki/Lolita_fashion "Lolita fashion"), and Ichigo "Ichiko" Shirayuri, a *[yankī](/wiki/Yank%C4%AB "Yankī")* (juvenile delinquent).

A live-action film adaptation was released on May 13, 2004. In the same year, a manga adaptation by [Yukio Kanesada](/w/index.php?title=Yukio_Kanesada&action=edit&redlink=1 "Yukio Kanesada (page does not exist)") [[ja](https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/%E3%81%8B%E3%81%AD%E3%81%95%E3%81%A0%E9%9B%AA%E7%B7%92 "ja:かねさだ雪緒")] was serialized in *[Betsucomi](/wiki/Betsucomi "Betsucomi")*. [Viz Media](/wiki/Viz_Media "Viz Media") licensed the novel for an English-language release in North America in 2006.[[6]](#cite_note-6)[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kamikaze_Girls&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

Momoko Ryugasaki is a high school student who admires the [Rococo](/wiki/Rococo "Rococo") style and [Lolita fashion](/wiki/Lolita_fashion "Lolita fashion"). In contrast to her idyllic lifestyle, she is forced to flee her hometown [Amagasaki](/wiki/Amagasaki "Amagasaki") to live in her grandmother's house in [Shimotsuma](/wiki/Shimotsuma,_Ibaraki "Shimotsuma, Ibaraki"), [Ibaraki Prefecture](/wiki/Ibaraki_Prefecture "Ibaraki Prefecture"), after her father, a former *[yakuza](/wiki/Yakuza "Yakuza")* member, ran into legal trouble from selling [counterfeit](/wiki/Counterfeit "Counterfeit") [Versace](/wiki/Versace "Versace") apparel. Initially, Momoko is excited to move closer to Tokyo, where her favorite clothing brand [Baby, the Stars Shine Bright](/wiki/Baby,_the_Stars_Shine_Bright_(brand) "Baby, the Stars Shine Bright (brand)") is located, but she is dismayed at discovering Shimotsuma is a rural, countryside town. When she runs out of spending money, she resorts to selling her father's remaining merchandise. Ichigo Shirayuri, a member of the [all-female biker gang](/wiki/Sukeban "Sukeban") Ponytails who goes by the name "Ichiko", visits Momoko frequently to purchase clothing for her gang. Despite their opposite personalities and interests, the girls develop an unlikely friendship.

When Momoko runs out of merchandise to sell, Ichiko takes her to *[pachinko](/wiki/Pachinko "Pachinko")* parlors for more spending money. Momoko hits the jackpot, which draws the attention from the manager, but Ryuji comes to their defense. Ichiko falls in love with him, but she learns that he is the fiancé of Akimi, the Ponytails' leader. When Momoko tells Ichigo about her day trips to visit the Baby, the Stars Shine Bright store in Tokyo, Ichiko mentions that she would like to visit Emma, an embroidery service located in the same area, as she plans on getting her *kamikaze* coat embroidered to honor Akimi's marriage and retirement from the Ponytails. When the two visit Tokyo, Momoko's embroidery work catches the attention of Akinori Isobe, the founder of Baby, the Stars Shine Bright. Meanwhile, to Ichiko's disappointment, Emma has permanently closed, but Momoko offers to embroider her jacket instead.

Isobe later contacts Momoko and commissions her to embroider a jumper skirt for the store's display. She completes the embroidery on both the jumper skirt and Ichiko's jacket in three days. While visiting Baby, the Stars Shine Bright to drop off the jumper skirt, Ichiko is asked to model for the brand's catalog after one of their models is injured. At Isobe's advice, Ichiko pursues a part-time modeling career. Momoko, on the other hand, turns down Isobe's suggestion to work for Baby, the Stars Shine Bright but decides to become Ichigo's manager.

Under Miko's new leadership, the Ponytails are unhappy with Ichiko becoming a model. Likewise, Ichiko is unsatisfied with the direction of the Ponytails and decides to quit the gang. As punishment for deserting the Ponytails, they challenge her to a fight, which Momoko overhears Ichiko accepting over the phone. Concerned for Ichiko, Momoko locates their fight and causes the Ponytails to flee by throwing her grandmother's scooter and water balloons at them. The two girls ride home on Ichiko's motorbike.

## Media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kamikaze_Girls&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Media")]

### Novels

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kamikaze_Girls&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Novels")]

*Kamikaze Girls* is written by [Novala Takemoto](/wiki/Novala_Takemoto "Novala Takemoto"). It is published by [Shogakukan](/wiki/Shogakukan "Shogakukan"). In 2004, the novel was re-released in *[bunkoban](/wiki/Bunkoban "Bunkoban")* paperback format. He followed up with a sequel, titled *Shimotsuma Monogatari: Kan: Yankī-chan to Rorīta-chan to Satsujin Jijō* in 2005. An audiobook of the second volume, narrated by Ayu Shoji, was released on April 23, 2025.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

[Viz Media](/wiki/Viz_Media "Viz Media") licensed the first novel for English-language distribution in North America. They re-released the novel in 2008.

| No. | Title | Original release date | English release date |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| 1 | *Kamikaze Girls* *Shimotsuma Monogatari: Yankī-chan to Rorīta-chan* (下妻物語 ヤンキーちゃんとロリータちゃん) | October 10, 2002 (2002-10-10) (hardcover) March 5, 2004 (2004-03-05) ([paperback](/wiki/Bunkoban "Bunkoban")) [4093861129](/wiki/Special:BookSources/4093861129 "Special:BookSources/4093861129") (hardcover) [4094080236](/wiki/Special:BookSources/4094080236 "Special:BookSources/4094080236") (paperback) | February 7, 2006 (2006-02-07) (hardcover) January 15, 2008 (2008-01-15) (paperback) [1421502690](/wiki/Special:BookSources/1421502690 "Special:BookSources/1421502690") (hardcover) [9781421513959](/wiki/Special:BookSources/9781421513959 "Special:BookSources/9781421513959") (paperback) |
| 2 | *Shimotsuma Monogatari: Kan: Yankī-chan to Rorīta-chan to Satsujin Jijō* (下妻物語 完 ヤンキーちゃんとロリータちゃんと殺人事件) | June 25, 2005 (2005-06-25) [4093861536](/wiki/Special:BookSources/4093861536 "Special:BookSources/4093861536") | — |

### Film

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kamikaze_Girls&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Film")]

A [live-action](/wiki/Live-action "Live-action") [film adaptation](/wiki/Film_adaptation "Film adaptation") of the novel directed by [Tetsuya Nakashima](/wiki/Tetsuya_Nakashima "Tetsuya Nakashima") premiered in Japan in May 2004. It starred [Kyoko Fukada](/wiki/Kyoko_Fukada "Kyoko Fukada") as Momoko and [Anna Tsuchiya](/wiki/Anna_Tsuchiya "Anna Tsuchiya") as Ichigo.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10) It was filmed in the town of [Shimotsuma](/wiki/Shimotsuma "Shimotsuma") in [Ibaraki Prefecture](/wiki/Ibaraki_Prefecture "Ibaraki Prefecture") in eastern Japan. Viz Media screened the film in select theaters in the United States in late 2005 under the title *Kamikaze Girls*.[[11]](#cite_note-Viz_Theater_PR-11) They released it on DVD with [hardcoded](/wiki/Hardcoded "Hardcoded") English subtitles in January 2006.[[12]](#cite_note-Viz_DVD_Manga_PR-12) The DVD extras include the original Japanese film trailers, an interview with the lead actors, and a music video featuring Anna Tsuchiya. Third Window Films released *Kamikaze Girls* on [Blu-ray](/wiki/Blu-ray "Blu-ray") in the United Kingdom in February 2010. The Blu-ray contains optional English subtitles, the same extras as the DVD, and the [short film](/wiki/Short_film "Short film") *Birth of Unicorn Ryuji*.[[13]](#cite_note-twf-13)

#### Cast

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kamikaze_Girls&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Cast")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/a/a5/Ceremony_one_year_before_the_start_of_new_4K%268K_satellite_broadcasting_2_%28cropped%29.png)](/wiki/File:Ceremony_one_year_before_the_start_of_new_4K%268K_satellite_broadcasting_2_(cropped).png)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b1/Anna_Tsuchiya_%28cropped%29.jpg/250px-Anna_Tsuchiya_%28cropped%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Anna_Tsuchiya_(cropped).jpg)

[Kyoko Fukada](/wiki/Kyoko_Fukada "Kyoko Fukada") (left, 2017) and [Anna Tsuchiya](/wiki/Anna_Tsuchiya "Anna Tsuchiya") (right, 2007) starred as Momoko and Ichigo in the live-action film adaptation respectively.

* [Kyoko Fukada](/wiki/Kyoko_Fukada "Kyoko Fukada") as Momoko Ryugasaki
* [Anna Tsuchiya](/wiki/Anna_Tsuchiya "Anna Tsuchiya") as Ichigo "Ichiko" Shirayuri
* [Hiroyuki Miyasako](/wiki/Hiroyuki_Miyasako "Hiroyuki Miyasako") as Momoko's father
* [Ryoko Shinohara](/wiki/Ryoko_Shinohara "Ryoko Shinohara") as Midori Saionji, Momoko's mother
* [Sadawo Abe](/wiki/Sadawo_Abe "Sadawo Abe") as "Unicorn" Ryuji
* [Yoshinori Okada](/wiki/Yoshinori_Okada "Yoshinori Okada") as Akinori Isobe
* [Eiko Koike](/wiki/Eiko_Koike "Eiko Koike") as Akimi, a gang member
* [Shin Yazawa](/wiki/Shin_Yazawa "Shin Yazawa") as Miko, a gang member
* [Yoshiyoshi Arakawa](/wiki/Yoshiyoshi_Arakawa "Yoshiyoshi Arakawa") as the grocery store manager
* [Katsuhisa Namase](/wiki/Katsuhisa_Namase "Katsuhisa Namase") as the pachinko parlor manager
* Hirotaro Honda as a [yakuza](/wiki/Yakuza "Yakuza") boss
* [Kirin Kiki](/wiki/Kirin_Kiki "Kirin Kiki") as Momoko's grandmother

#### Crew

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kamikaze_Girls&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Crew")]

* Director – [Tetsuya Nakashima](/wiki/Tetsuya_Nakashima "Tetsuya Nakashima")
* Director of Photography – Shoichi Ato
* Production Designer – Towako Kuwashima
* Animation – Yojiro Nishimura ([Studio 4°C](/wiki/Studio_4%C2%B0C "Studio 4°C"))
* Music – [Yoko Kanno](/wiki/Yoko_Kanno "Yoko Kanno")[[13]](#cite_note-twf-13)

#### Reception

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kamikaze_Girls&action=edit&section=7 "Edit section: Reception")]

*Kamikaze Girls* was awarded Best Film, Best Director, Best Actress, and two other awards at the [26th Yokohama Film Festival](/wiki/26th_Yokohama_Film_Festival "26th Yokohama Film Festival").[[11]](#cite_note-Viz_Theater_PR-11) It also won Best Film and Best Director at the [14th Japan Film Professional Awards](/wiki/14th_Japan_Film_Professional_Awards "14th Japan Film Professional Awards").[[14]](#cite_note-14) For her performance in the film, Anna Tsuchiya was named Best New Actress at the [Awards of the Japanese Academy](/wiki/Japan_Academy_Film_Prize "Japan Academy Film Prize"), the [Blue Ribbon Awards](/wiki/Blue_Ribbon_Awards "Blue Ribbon Awards"), and the [Hochi Film Awards](/wiki/Hochi_Film_Award "Hochi Film Award").[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)

[Review aggregation](/wiki/Review_aggregation "Review aggregation") website [Rotten Tomatoes](/wiki/Rotten_Tomatoes "Rotten Tomatoes") gives *Kamikaze Girls* an approval rating of 62%, based on 29 reviews, with an [average](/wiki/Weighted_mean "Weighted mean") rating of 6.1/10.[[18]](#cite_note-rt-18) The film also has a 56/100 [average](/wiki/Weighted_arithmetic_mean "Weighted arithmetic mean") ("mixed or average reviews") on the review aggregator [Metacritic](/wiki/Metacritic "Metacritic").[[19]](#cite_note-meta-19)

#### Box office

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kamikaze_Girls&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Box office")]

Released on May 29, 2004, *Kamikaze Girls* debuted at No. 4 on its opening weekend (behind *[Crimson Rivers II](/wiki/Crimson_Rivers_II:_Angels_of_the_Apocalypse "Crimson Rivers II: Angels of the Apocalypse")*, *[Troy](/wiki/Troy_(film) "Troy (film)")*, and *[Crying Out Love in the Center of the World](/wiki/Crying_Out_Love_in_the_Center_of_the_World "Crying Out Love in the Center of the World")*).[[20]](#cite_note-20)

### Manga

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kamikaze_Girls&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Manga")]

The manga adaptation of *Kamikaze Girls* was illustrated by Yukio Kanesada and serialized in [Shogakukan](/wiki/Shogakukan "Shogakukan")'s *[Betsucomi](/wiki/Betsucomi "Betsucomi")* magazine in 2004.[[21]](#cite_note-21) Shogakukan collected the chapters into a single *tankōbon* (bound volume) and published it in June 2004.[[22]](#cite_note-22) The manga's storyline is a condensed version of the original novel and only takes up about half of the volume; the latter half contains a bonus story in which Ichigo falls in love with the twin brother of the boy she loved in the novel. [Viz Media](/wiki/Viz_Media "Viz Media") licensed the manga for an English-language release in North America.[[12]](#cite_note-Viz_DVD_Manga_PR-12) A preview first appeared in the November 2005 issue of their *[Shojo Beat](/wiki/Shojo_Beat "Shojo Beat")* magazine.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] Viz published the full volume on February 7, 2006.[[12]](#cite_note-Viz_DVD_Manga_PR-12)[[23]](#cite_note-23)
